creator: pfg@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 159650
FORTIFY_SOURCE enabled by default (WIP)

Hello;

As part of this year's GSoC, Oliver Pinter has ported the FORTIFY_SOURCE infrastructure from bionic's libc. This libc feature lets us detect buffer overflows during compile time or runtime. We have run builds with both gcc-4.2.1 and clang but we are still missing two types of build:

1) Build with newer gcc: this will detect more vulnerabilities that are not possible with the limited functionality in gcc-4.2.

2) Run tests on a fortified system (clang): this would help us detect issues that are not detected by compile time checks.

FORTIFY_SOURCE=1 never produces false positives. Nothing good comes from buffer overflows so we would like to eventually get to the point where we can enable FORTIFY_SOURCE by default but we have to detect all the issues we can before enabling it.

Oliver Pinter has experience with Jenkins and could join the team to undergo such tests. We have also discussed the possibility of adding build targets for the different sanitizers that come with clang. The code is available on Code Review(D3043).
